claim: Skills package specialized domain expertise (frontend design, API architecture, database optimization) directly into agents, enabling them to write better code by default
actionable: Create and use skills to inject engineering expertise into agents—don't rely on base models which write plausible but often poor code without explicit guidance.
source_title: 7 CLAUDE CODE Skills Every Beginner NEEDS To Master

claim: Frontend Design skill provides specific UI/UX expertise to agents, solving the common problem that beginners struggle with aesthetics and user experience
actionable: Use Anthropic's official Frontend Design skill when building UIs without design experience—it provides structured guidance for creating professional interfaces.
source_title: 7 CLAUDE CODE Skills Every Beginner NEEDS To Master

claim: Superpowers library contains interconnected skills (brainstorming, planning, debugging) that work together in pipelines for comprehensive feature development
actionable: Use the Superpowers skill library which hooks together—start with brainstorming, move to planning, then implementation, debugging—rather than assembling ad-hoc skills.
source_title: 7 CLAUDE CODE Skills Every Beginner NEEDS To Master

claim: Error handling expertise in skills makes agents perform better and debug faster; well-categorized errors improve model performance more than vague error messages
actionable: Use error handling skills to implement consistent, categorized error patterns—specific errors help agents self-correct faster than generic error messages.
source_title: 7 CLAUDE CODE Skills Every Beginner NEEDS To Master

claim: Custom commands are reusable prompts, sub-agents are isolated context windows for specific problems, skills give expertise to your main agent
actionable: Use custom commands for reusable prompts, sub-agents for isolated problems, and skills for adding expertise—choose based on whether you need isolation or integrated expertise.
source_title: 7 CLAUDE CODE Skills Every Beginner NEEDS To Master
